text: The coworker had taken the blame for the missed deadline and they had not corrected it, had not even really meant to say nothing, but saying nothing was itself a decision they had made, and now the coworker's desk was being cleared out on Friday

They found the library book wedged behind the radiator, eighteen months overdue, its cover warped and pages yellowed from the heat, and they slid it into a return bag without making eye contact with the librarian
The birthday card was still in the glove compartment, unsigned, the envelope unsealed, and now it was March and the birthday had been in November
Their mother had called four times that week and each time they had watched the phone ring on the counter and then set it face down and gone back to whatever small thing they were doing
The dog pressed its nose to the window when they picked up the car keys, and they said they would be right back, but they were going to dinner and a movie and would not be back for five hours
At the checkout they noticed the cashier had not scanned the expensive cheese, and they watched the total appear on the screen and said nothing and put their card in the reader
The child had drawn the picture specifically for them and they had set it on the counter and a glass of water had been placed on top of it and now the crayon was bleeding into shapelessness
They sat in the parked car for eleven minutes after arriving home, rehearsing how to explain why the savings account was empty, and then they rehearsed it again with different words
The old woman next door had been bringing them soup every week since the surgery, and they had never once brought anything back, not even the empty containers
They deleted the text thread before their partner could see it, not because anything had happened but because of the way the conversation had started to lean, the way they had let it lean
The volunteer coordinator had left three voicemails about Saturday's shift and they had not returned a single one and now it was Sunday
Their sister had asked them to water the plants while she was away and two of the orchids were now brown stalks in dry soil and the funeral was tomorrow and there was no way to replace them in time
They had promised the intern a recommendation letter and it had been six weeks and the application deadline was tonight and they had not started writing it
The dent in the neighbor's car was small enough that no one would know, and they backed out of the parking space and drove home and checked the bumper three times that evening
During the toast at the retirement dinner they had made a joke that landed wrong and the retiree's smile had flickered and not come back for the rest of the evening
They threw away the leftovers their grandmother had packed in the old Tupperware, and the Tupperware too, because it smelled, and now she was asking about the container every time they visited
The scholarship money had been meant for textbooks but they had spent most of it in the first week on things they could not now name or find in the apartment
They had told the child that the goldfish went to a better pond and the child had believed it completely and now drew pictures of the fish swimming in a big river with other fish

When the group project received the award, they stood on stage knowing they had written perhaps two paragraphs of the forty-page report, and the applause felt like it was aimed at someone standing behind them

The friend had driven four hours to visit and they had spent most of the evening glancing at their phone, and when the friend left the next morning the hug at the door lasted longer than any of their conversations had
They returned the dress after wearing it to the wedding, tags carefully retucked, and the clerk processed the refund without question, and they walked out of the store faster than necessary
The cat had been meowing at the back door for twenty minutes in the rain before they noticed, and when they opened it the cat walked past them without its usual greeting and sat alone under the table
They ate the last slice of cake that had someone else's name written on the box in marker, standing over the kitchen sink at two in the morning

The email from the charity showed a photo of the child they had sponsored, holding a sign that said thank you, and they had cancelled the monthly donation three months ago to pay for a streaming subscription
They had not visited the grave since the funeral and it was now autumn and the cemetery was only twelve minutes from their apartment and they drove past the exit every day on the way to work
The babysitter mentioned that the toddler had said mama fourteen times during the three hours, and they were at a happy hour they had not needed to attend
Their roommate's yogurt was the only thing in the fridge and they ate it and replaced it with a different brand and hoped no one would notice, but the roommate always bought the same brand
The teacher had pulled them aside to say their son had been eating lunch alone every day that month, and they realized they had not asked the boy a single question about school since September
They forwarded the confidential email to the wrong person and then spent forty minutes trying to recall the message and crafting an explanation that rearranged the sequence of events

The thank-you note from the patient's family arrived at the clinic and praised their quick thinking, but the truth was they had hesitated for nearly a full minute before acting, standing frozen by the monitor
They had told the mechanic to use the cheaper part and now the car was making the same sound again and their partner was driving it to work every morning on the highway

The potluck dish they brought was store-bought, transferred into a home pan, and when someone asked for the recipe they invented one on the spot, adding details about oven temperature and resting time
They skipped the funeral to go to the interview, and got the job, and every time someone congratulated them the date on the offer letter reminded them of where they had not been that afternoon
The tree their father had planted the year they were born was in the way of the new garage and they had it cut down on a Tuesday while no one else was home
They told the dentist they flossed daily and the dentist said nothing but wrote something down and the hygienist's expression did not change either
The child's recital was the same night as the game and they had chosen the game and the child had looked out at the audience only once from the stage and then not again
They had copied three paragraphs from an obscure journal and changed enough words that the plagiarism checker returned clean, and the professor had written excellent original thinking in the margin

The stray they had been feeding stopped appearing at the back porch after they put up the fence, and sometimes at dusk they opened the gate and left a bowl out anyway, though it was always untouched by morning
Their brother had lent them the money without asking when it would be repaid, and it had been two years, and at Christmas dinner the brother mentioned casually that he was picking up extra shifts
The donated coat still had a twenty-dollar bill in the pocket and they almost called the charity but instead they bought lunch and sat with the receipt for a long time afterward
